pubmed_links: [Moon et al. (2024) Knockout of intellectual disability-linked Hs6st2 decreases 6-O-sulfation, impairs dendritic spines and memory](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38015989/); [Yabe et al. (2005) Developmental and regional expression of heparan sulfate sulfotransferase genes in the mouse brain](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/15944372/); [Alexander et al. (2022) Autism, heparan sulfate and potential interventions](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/35307405/)
summary: HS6ST3 encodes one of three heparan sulfate 6-O-sulfotransferases with temporally regulated expression in developing cerebellar Purkinje cells and cortical pyramidal neurons; while HS6ST3 itself lacks direct published disease associations, the paralog HS6ST2 is linked to intellectual disability, HS6ST3's chromosomal locus 13q32-q33 falls within a region showing linkage to bipolar disorder and schizophrenia, and heparan sulfate deficiency broadly has been implicated in autism spectrum disorder pathology. (novel candidate)
